name: The Institutional Cover-Up
description: An investigation exposes how a powerful institution — police, church, agency, corporation, school — hid wrongdoing and protected itself instead of the people it harmed.

name: History, Retold
description: A narrative rebuilds a past event, era, or figure from the record — revising what we thought we knew about how it really happened.

name: Unsolved Murders & Cold Cases
description: A killing that was never solved (or never properly closed) gets reopened years later, with reporters chasing overlooked evidence and living suspects.

name: Abuse of Power
description: Someone with authority — a doctor, coach, boss, officer, guru, mogul — exploits that trust, and reporting follows the trail of victims.

name: Personal Mysteries & Obsessions
description: A host chases a private, human-scale puzzle — a lost person, an unanswered question, a strange story — often as much about the searcher as the answer.

name: Fraud, Scams & Ponzis
description: Money is the crime scene — fake companies, Ponzi schemes, crypto vanishings, and cooked books that unravel when the cash runs out.

name: Survivor Testimony
description: The story is carried in the first person by the people who lived it — survivors of harm, incarceration, or catastrophe speaking for themselves.

name: True Crime, Deep-Dive
description: The classic longform crime narrative — one case dissected episode by episode, tracing investigators, suspects, and the toll of the crime.

name: The Charismatic Con Artist
description: A magnetic liar wins trust, builds a fantasy, and leaves ruin behind — the seducer-grifter at the center of the story.

name: Media & Internet Culture
description: How platforms, tabloids, virality, and online rabbit holes shape belief and behavior — and sometimes manufacture the story itself.

name: Fiction Wearing Journalism's Clothes
description: Scripted audio drama built to sound like a documentary or investigation — found tapes, reporter narrators, and mysteries that never happened.

name: Racial Injustice & Civil Rights
description: Histories and investigations of racial harm — from civil-rights-era cold cases to present-day systemic inequity and the fight against it.

name: Disappearances & Missing Persons
description: Someone vanishes — and the search for what happened drives the whole series, whether the person is ever found or not.

name: Political Scandal
description: Power, corruption, and the machinery of government — a scandal, coup, or crooked deal reconstructed to show how politics really works.

name: Building (and Breaking) an Empire
description: The rise and reckoning of companies and moguls — how empires get built, and how ambition, fraud, or hubris tears them down.

name: Music History & the Stories Behind the Songs
description: How records, artists, and scenes came to be — the craft, culture, and myth of music, sometimes tangled with crime or politics.

name: Police Misconduct
description: Cops, prosecutors, and the systems around them abuse their authority — framing, cover-ups, violence, and the fight to expose it.

name: Wrongful Conviction
description: Someone may be imprisoned for a crime they didn't commit, and the reporting reexamines the evidence that put them there.

name: Family Secrets
description: A buried truth inside a family — a hidden relative, a crime, a lie — surfaces and rewrites the whole story of who people are.

name: Tech Gone Wrong
description: Technology as the threat — hacks, crypto, surveillance, radicalizing algorithms, and inventions that outrun their makers.

name: Spies & Secret Intelligence
description: Espionage, covert operations, and the shadow world of intelligence agencies and the assets who work in it.

name: Serial Killers
description: The hunt for — or reckoning with — a killer who struck again and again, and the wreckage they left across a community.

name: War & Conflict
description: Reporting from and about war — its front lines, its aftermath, and the people and decisions that shaped it.

name: Science & Wonder
description: Curiosity-driven journeys into how the world works — animals, space, the mind, and the edges of what we understand.

name: Cults & High-Control Groups
description: Inside groups that isolate and control their members — the leaders who build them and the survivors who escape.

name: Religion & Faith
description: Belief as a force in people's lives — churches, prophets, and the line between devotion and exploitation.

name: Sports, Beyond the Game
description: Sports as a lens on money, power, race, and culture — the scandals and stories behind the competition.

name: Medical Harm
description: Medicine as the source of injury — dangerous doctors, negligent institutions, and patients harmed by the systems meant to heal them.

name: Environment & Climate
description: The natural world under threat — disaster, pollution, and the human decisions behind ecological harm.

name: Immigration & Borders
description: Stories of migration, borders, and belonging across the Americas and beyond.
